---
title: "Practical protocol"
format: html
---

| Study Overview |   |
|-------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Study name | HEADWISE - Head Trauma Evaluation Algorithm for Deploying Warranted Imaging Safely and Efficiently |
| Objective | Evaluate the diagnostic performance and safety profile of a new Choosing Wisely algorithm for MHT management, with the goal of reducing CT overuse in emergency settings. |
| Study Sites | Aalborg University Hospital, Aarhus University Hospital, Esbjerg Hospital, Kolding Hospital, Hospital Sønderjylland, Odense University Hospital, Slagelse Hospital, Herlev Hospital, Bispebjerg and Frederiksberg Hospital, and Amager and Hvidovre Hospital |
| Study Period | 01.XX.202X - 01.XX.202X |
| Primary Endpoint | Proportion undergoing neurosurgery |
| Secondary Endpoints | Mortality, survival without readmission, proportion receiving CT of the brain, economic consequences, and related measures. |

| Roles and Responsibilities |   |   |
|-------------------------|------------------------|------------------------|
| Principal Investigator | Rozh Al-Mashhadi | Overall study oversight, data quality |
| Site Coordinator |  | Daily workflow management, staff training |
| Data Collector | Julie Bjørn Bay | Data extraction from registers |
| Clinician |  | Patient screening, guideline adherence |
| Statistician |  | Data analysis plan |
|  |  |  |

**Study Workflow**

**Step 1 – Patient Screening**

-   Identify adult patients presenting with **mild head trauma (GCS 14–15)**

-   Apply **inclusion/exclusion criteria**

-   Hand eligible patients Patient Information

**Step 2 – Guideline Assignment**

-   Check which guideline is active at your site **based on stepped-wedge schedule**

-   Use flowchart to decide whether a CT scan is indicated

**Step 3 – Data Collection**

-   If patient is eligible assign the diagnosis code **DS098C - Let hovedtraume (GCS 14-15)**

-   If patient is not eligible use another diagnosis code

-   If patient want to opt out of data analysis, the patient should register this in REDCap or send us an email (see patient information)

![](images/stepped_wedge.png)
